commonProblems.h2: Common Lexus Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure – Why Lexus V6 Engines Develop Rattle at Idle
problems.affectedModels: Lexus IS350, GS350, RX350 (2007–2015, engine codes 2GR-FKS, 4GR-FSE, 3GR-FSE)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioners lose pressure over time due to oil sludge buildup or delayed oil changes, allowing the timing chain to stretch and create rattling noise at cold start — if ignored, chain jump can cause valve-to-piston contact .
problems.recommendation: If your Lexus V6 shows early tensioner noise but no check-engine light or performance loss, the Minor tier can be cost-effective — but only if you commit to strict 5,000-mile synthetic oil changes thereafter. Once chain stretch exceeds manufacturer tolerance (confirmed via Techstream diagnostic), the Intermediate service is the last viable repair before catastrophic failure. For IS/GS/RX models valued under £8,000, a rebuilt engine from EngineMarket's network typically delivers better ROI than a £2,450 timing overhaul, with the added security of a full warranty.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 2GR-FKS / 4GR-FSE engine prices for your Lexus model

problems.h4: Water Pump & Coolant System Failure – Preventing Overheat Damage on Lexus Petrol Engines
problems.affectedModels: Lexus IS250, ES250, NX200t (2010–2020, engine codes 2AR-FSE, 8AR-FTS)
problems.rootCause: Plastic impeller water pumps develop micro-cracks in the housing or bearing seal failure, leading to coolant leakage onto the timing cover; prolonged leakage causes corrosion of the tensioner mount and eventual overheating .
problems.recommendation: Lexus water pump failures are often preventable with proactive replacement at 70k miles. If you see pink coolant residue around the timing cover or notice intermittent temperature gauge fluctuations, the Minor tier is a smart investment. However, if the engine has overheated (check for white exhaust smoke or oil/coolant mixing), do not attempt a pump-only fix — the head gasket is likely compromised, making Full Replacement the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: → Get instant quotes for rebuilt 2AR-FSE / 8AR-FTS engines with upgraded cooling systems

problems.h4: Excessive Oil Consumption – Piston Ring & PCV System Failure on Lexus 2.5L Engines
problems.affectedModels: Lexus IS250, GS250, ES250 (2006–2017, engine codes 2AR-FSE, 4GR-FSE)
problems.rootCause: Early-production piston rings have insufficient tension, allowing oil to bypass into the combustion chamber; combined with PCV valve clogging, this creates accelerated oil burn and carbon buildup on intake valves .
problems.recommendation: If your Lexus consumes less than 0.5L per 1,000 miles and compression tests are within 10% across cylinders, the Minor service can restore acceptable operation. However, once consumption exceeds 1L/1,000 miles or you detect blue exhaust smoke, the piston rings have likely worn beyond repair — at that point, Full Replacement delivers superior value versus a £2,750 ring job that may not resolve underlying cylinder wear.
problems.cta: → Compare prices for rebuilt 2AR-FSE engines with updated piston ring specifications

problems.group: Hybrid Inverter & MG Motor Sync Faults
problems.h4: Hybrid Inverter & MG Motor Sync Faults – Power Loss on Lexus Hybrid Models
problems.affectedModels: Lexus CT200h, IS300h, RX450h (2010–2022, hybrid codes A25A-FXS, 2ZR-FXE, 2GR-FXE)
problems.rootCause: Inverter coolant pump failure or MG1 motor bearing wear disrupts the precise synchronization between petrol engine and electric motor, triggering "Check Hybrid System" warnings and reduced power mode .
problems.recommendation: Hybrid faults on Lexus models are highly diagnostic-dependent. If your vehicle shows only P0A93 (inverter coolant pump) with no drivability issues, the Minor repair is cost-effective. However, if you experience "Check Hybrid System" warnings with reduced power or unusual transaxle noise, do not delay — internal MG bearing failure can cascade to inverter damage, making Full Replacement the only viable option. EngineMarket's hybrid-specialist network ensures proper HV safety protocols and ECU synchronization.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt A25A-FXS / 2ZR-FXE hybrid powertrains with upgraded inverters

problems.group: EGR Cooler Leak & DPF Regeneration Failure
problems.h4: EGR Cooler Leak & DPF Regeneration Failure – Diesel-Specific Issues on Lexus 2.2L Engines
problems.affectedModels: Lexus IS220d, GS220d, RX270 (2008–2015, engine codes 1AD-FTV, 2AD-FHV)
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler internal cracks allow coolant to mix with exhaust gases, causing white smoke and DPF clogging; combined with short-trip UK driving patterns, this accelerates soot accumulation and regeneration failure .
problems.recommendation: Lexus diesel owners in urban UK environments should prioritize preventative EGR system maintenance. If your vehicle shows occasional white smoke but no coolant loss, the Minor cleaning can restore function. However, confirmed coolant in the exhaust or repeated DPF warning lights indicate internal cooler failure — at that point, Full Replacement delivers better long-term value than a £1,850 intermediate repair that may not resolve underlying material fatigue.

engineTypes.h2: Lexus Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Lexus engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Lexus Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price — ideal for addressing common Lexus failure points like timing chain tensioner wear or hybrid water pump degradation.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,800 – £5,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Lexus Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as uprated timing chain kits for 2AR-FSE/2GR-FKS engines and reinforced bearing shells for high-mileage AD diesel units – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Lexus Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available — suitable for older CT 200h or IS 220d models where a full rebuild isn't economical.

types.title: Lexus Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models like the LC 500 or LS 500h, or owners wanting a zero‑mile engine with hybrid inverter calibration included.

types.title: Lexus Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models where a full recon isn't economical — ideal for pre‑2015 RX 350 or GS 250 owners seeking a cost‑effective interim fix.

types.title: Lexus Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Lexus specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty — essential for hybrid powertrains requiring specialist inverter sync calibration and coolant system bleeding.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £450 – £1,200 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Lexus engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Lexus model today.

engineSizes.h2: Lexus Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model

items.title: Lexus 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2‑litre D‑4D diesel family powers Lexus's early diesel offerings, primarily the IS220d and GS220d saloons from the mid‑2000s to early‑2010s. These 1AD‑FTV and 2AD‑FHV units deliver strong torque but are prone to EGR cooler leaks and DPF regeneration failures in UK stop‑start conditions — the most common triggers for replacement demand across the brand's diesel range. Engine code(s): 1AD-FTV, 2AD-FHV Compatible models (UK): IS220d, GS220d (2005–2012) Production years: 2005–2012 Common failure points: EGR cooler internal cracking, DPF clogging from short trips, timing chain tensioner wear on high‑mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Lexus 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Lexus 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The compact 1.5‑litre M15A‑FKS three‑cylinder powers the entry‑level UX150 and hybrid UX250h, representing Lexus's newest small‑capacity petrol architecture. While relatively new to the UK market with strong reliability data so far, direct‑injection carbon buildup on intake valves is the primary long‑term consideration for owners planning extended ownership. Engine code(s): M15A-FKS, M15A-FXS (hybrid variant) Compatible models (UK): UX150, UX250h (2019–present) Production years: 2019–present Common failure points: Carbon accumulation on intake valves (direct injection), occasional VVT‑i actuator rattle on early production
items.cta: Compare Lexus 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: Lexus's 1.8‑litre petrol units appear in two distinct forms: the older naturally‑aspirated 1AZ‑FE found in early IS200/GS200 models, and the hybrid‑optimised 2ZR‑FXE used across the CT200h and early UX hybrids. The non‑hybrid 1.8L is largely phased out, while the hybrid variant remains in production with excellent longevity when coolant system maintenance is upheld. Engine code(s): 1AZ-FE, 2ZR-FXE, 1NZ-FXE (hybrid) Compatible models (UK): IS200, CT200h, UX250h (1999–present) Production years: 1999–present Common failure points: 1AZ – oil consumption from piston ring wear; hybrid variants – inverter coolant pump failure, MG motor bearing wear
items.cta: Compare Lexus 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0‑litre displacement spans multiple Lexus generations, from the naturally‑aspirated 3ZR‑FE in early compact models to the turbocharged 8AR‑FTS powering the NX200t and RX200t. The turbo variant delivers strong performance but introduces complexity: water pump housing cracks and turbo wastegate rattle are the most frequently reported failure points driving replacement enquiries in the UK. Engine code(s): 3ZR-FE, 8AR-FTS, 1GR-FE (4.0L V6 – listed for reference only) Compatible models (UK): IS200, NX200t, RX200t, GX400 (2000–present) Production years: 2000–present Common failure points: 8AR-FTS – water pump housing micro‑cracks, turbo wastegate actuator wear; 3ZR – VVT‑i oil control valve clogging
items.cta: Compare Lexus 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5‑litre displacement is Lexus's most versatile petrol size, appearing in naturally‑aspirated V6 form (4GR‑FSE, 5GR‑FSE) for IS250/GS250 and as the modern inline‑four A25A‑FKS/FXS in NX250, UX250h and ES250. Oil consumption from early‑spec piston rings and timing chain tensioner wear on the V6 variants represent the dominant failure patterns that drive replacement demand across this high‑volume displacement. Engine code(s): 2AR-FSE, 4GR-FSE, 5GR-FSE, A25A-FKS, A25A-FXS Compatible models (UK): IS250, GS250, ES250, NX250, UX250h (2005–present) Production years: 2005–present Common failure points: 2AR/4GR – excessive oil consumption (piston ring tension), timing chain tensioner fatigue; A25A – carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
items.cta: Compare Lexus 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0‑litre 3GR‑FSE V6 powers the GS300 and IS300 saloons from the mid‑2000s, offering smooth refinement with D‑4S direct and port injection. While generally robust, VVT‑i actuator wear and oil leaks from valve cover gaskets are the most common age‑related issues that prompt owners to seek replacement units in the UK marketplace. Engine code(s): 3GR-FSE Compatible models (UK): GS300, IS300 (2005–2012) Production years: 2005–2012 Common failure points: VVT‑i actuator rattle, valve cover gasket oil seepage, occasional water pump bearing wear
items.cta: Compare Lexus 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Lexus's flagship 3.5‑litre 2GR family (2GR‑FKS petrol, 2GR‑FXS/FXE hybrid) powers the IS350, GS350, RX350 and RX450h across multiple generations. Timing chain tensioner fatigue on pre‑2015 units and water pump housing cracks are the predominant failure points that drive replacement enquiries, with hybrid variants adding inverter coolant system considerations. Engine code(s): 2GR-FKS, 2GR-FXS, 2GR-FXE Compatible models (UK): IS350, GS350, RX350, RX450h, LS460 (2006–present) Production years: 2006–present Common failure points: Timing chain tensioner wear (pre‑2015), water pump housing micro‑cracks, hybrid variants – inverter coolant pump failure
items.cta: Compare Lexus 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally‑aspirated 5.0‑litre 2UR‑FSE V8 powers the LC500, LS500 and RC F performance models, representing Lexus's pinnacle petrol architecture. Exceptionally robust when maintained, the primary replacement driver is oil pump drive gear wear on very early LC500 production (2017–2018), though this remains a relatively low‑volume failure pattern. Engine code(s): 2UR-FSE Compatible models (UK): LC500, LS500, RC F (2017–present) Production years: 2017–present Common failure points: Oil pump drive gear wear (early LC500), occasional exhaust manifold bolt fracture on high‑mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Lexus 5.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.7‑litre 3UR‑FE V8 is exclusive to the LX570 flagship SUV, offering legendary off‑road durability with minimal electronic complexity. Replacement demand is exceptionally low due to the engine's robust design, with exhaust manifold bolt fatigue representing the most commonly reported age‑related issue among UK owners. Engine code(s): 3UR-FE Compatible models (UK): LX570 (2008–2021) Production years: 2008–2021 Common failure points: Exhaust manifold bolt fracture (age‑related), generally minimal failure points given robust design
items.cta: Compare Lexus 5.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Lexus 1.8L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Lexus's pioneering 1.8‑litre hybrid system (1NZ‑FXE) debuted in the CT200h and remains in production for select global markets, pairing a naturally‑aspirated petrol engine with dual electric motors for exceptional urban efficiency. Replacement is specialist‑led due to high‑voltage safety requirements, with inverter coolant pump failure representing the most common trigger for powertrain enquiries. System / motor code(s): 1NZ-FXE (petrol), P410 transaxle Compatible models (UK): CT200h (2011–2020) Production years: 2011–2020 Important notes: Battery health inspection critical before engine swap; inverter coolant system must be pressure‑tested
items.cta: Enquire about Lexus 1.8L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Lexus 2.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The modern 2.5‑litre hybrid architecture (A25A‑FXS) powers the UX250h, NX350h and ES300h, featuring Lexus's fourth‑generation hybrid system with improved thermal management and reduced component count. While failure rates remain low, hybrid system synchronization faults and inverter coolant pump wear are the primary triggers for specialist replacement enquiries in the UK. System / motor code(s): A25A-FXS (petrol), P610/P710 transaxle Compatible models (UK): UX250h, NX350h, ES300h (2018–present) Production years: 2018–present Important notes: HV ECU synchronization required post‑fitment; battery pack health assessment recommended
items.cta: Enquire about Lexus 2.5L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Lexus 3.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Lexus's flagship 3.5‑litre hybrid system (2GR‑FXE) combines the refined 2GR V6 with dual electric motors in the RX450h and LS600h, delivering seamless power with exceptional refinement. Replacement is highly specialist due to complex power‑split device calibration, with inverter coolant pump failure and MG motor bearing wear representing the most common failure triggers. System / motor code(s): 2GR-FXE (petrol), P410/P412 transaxle Compatible models (UK): RX450h, LS600h (2009–present) Production years: 2009–present Important notes: Power‑split device calibration essential; full HV system diagnostic required pre‑ and post‑installation Closing line: Not sure which engine size your Lexus needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Lexus specialists. items.title: Lexus Diesel Engines
items.description: Short descriptor: Lexus's diesel range is focused on the 2.2‑litre D‑4D family, offered primarily in the IS220d and GS220d saloons from 2005–2012, with replacement enquiries driven by EGR cooler and DPF regeneration issues in UK urban driving conditions. Common Lexus Diesel Engine Families: 1AD-FTV – 2.2L D‑4D for IS220d/GS220d, EGR cooler cracking risk 2AD-FHV – Updated 2.2L D‑4D with DPF, timing chain tensioner wear 2AD-FTV – Early non‑DPF variant, similar failure patterns to 2AD-FHV Found In: IS220d (XE20, 2005–2011) – 1AD-FTV, 2AD-FHV GS220d (S190, 2006–2011) – 1AD-FTV, 2AD-FHV RX270 (AL10, 2009–2015) – 2AD-FHV (limited UK availability) Known For (Brand‑wide): Strong low‑end torque ideal for motorway cruising and towing EGR cooler internal leaks driving coolant loss and white smoke symptoms DPF clogging on short‑journey UK usage patterns accelerating replacement demand ~15% of Lexus engine replacement enquiries in the UK are diesel‑related Typical Diesel Models (UK): IS220d SE GS220d Sport RX270 SE-L IS220d Premium
items.cta: Get quotes for Lexus Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Lexus Petrol Engines
items.description: Short descriptor: Lexus petrol engines span compact 1.5L three‑cylinders to flagship 5.7L V8s, with the 2.5L and 3.5L families dominating UK replacement demand due to timing chain tensioner wear, water pump housing cracks, and oil consumption issues on high‑mileage examples. Common Lexus Petrol Engine Families: A25A-FKS/FXS – 2.5L Dynamic Force for NX/UX/ES, intake valve carbon buildup 2AR-FSE – 2.5L D‑4S for IS250/GS250, piston ring oil consumption issues 2GR-FKS/FXS – 3.5L V6 for IS350/GS350/RX350, pre‑2015 tensioner fatigue 8AR-FTS – 2.0L turbo for NX200t/RX200t, water pump housing cracks 2UR-FSE – 5.0L V8 for LC500/LS500, exceptional robustness minimal failures Found In: IS200/IS250/IS350 (XE10/XE20/XE30, 1999–present) – 1AZ-FE, 2AR-FSE, 4GR-FSE, 2GR-FKS GS250/GS300/GS350 (S190/S191/L10, 2005–2020) – 2AR-FSE, 3GR-FSE, 2GR-FKS RX350/RX450h (AL10/GL10/GL20, 2003–present) – 2GR-FKS, 2GR-FXS NX200t/NX250/NX350h (AZ10/AZ20, 2014–present) – 8AR-FTS, A25A-FKS, A25A-FXS UX200/UX250h (MZAA10, 2018–present) – M15A-FKS, A25A-FXS LC500/LS500 (URZ100/USF50, 2017–present) – 2UR-FSE LX570 (URJ200, 2008–2021) – 3UR-FE 5.7L V8 Known For (Brand‑wide): Exceptional refinement and smooth power delivery across all displacements Timing chain tensioner wear on pre‑2015 V6 units driving cold‑start rattles Water pump housing micro‑cracks on turbo petrol engines causing coolant leaks ~85% of Lexus engine replacement enquiries in the UK are petrol or hybrid‑petrol related Typical Petrol Models (UK): IS250 SE-L GS350 F Sport RX350 Premium NX200t F Sport UX250h Takumi LC500 Sport+

items.title: Lexus Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: Short descriptor: Lexus pioneered self‑charging hybrid technology in the premium segment, with Full Hybrid (HEV) and Plug‑in Hybrid (PHEV) systems now available across CT, UX, NX, RX, ES and LS ranges – replacement demand is specialist‑led due to high‑voltage safety requirements and inverter/MG motor integration. #### Lexus Full Hybrid (HEV) Systems Common Lexus HEV Engine/Families: 1NZ-FXE – 1.8L hybrid for CT200h, inverter coolant pump failure common 2ZR-FXE – Updated 1.8L hybrid for UX250h, improved thermal management A25A-FXS – 2.5L Dynamic Force hybrid for NX350h/ES300h, MG bearing wear 2GR-FXE/FXS – 3.5L V6 hybrid for RX450h/LS600h, power‑split calibration critical How Lexus Full Hybrid Systems Work (brand‑wide): Petrol engine and dual electric motors share power via planetary gearset seamlessly Regenerative braking recharges battery without plug‑in requirement for urban efficiency Battery health inspection and inverter coolant test essential during engine replacement Found In: CT200h (ZWA10, 2011–2020) – 1NZ-FXE HEV UX250h (MZAA10, 2018–present) – A25A-FXS HEV NX350h (AZ20, 2021–present) – A25A-FXS HEV ES300h (XZ10, 2018–present) – A25A-FXS HEV RX450h (GYL10/GL10/GL20, 2009–present) – 2GR-FXE/FXS HEV LS600h (UVF45, 2007–2017) – 2UR-FSE + electric motors #### Lexus Plug‑in Hybrid (PHEV) Systems Common Lexus PHEV Engine/Families: A25A-FXS + PHEV – NX450h+/RX450h+, ~40 miles electric‑only WLTP range How Lexus Plug‑in Hybrid Systems Work (brand‑wide): Larger lithium‑ion battery enables extended electric‑only driving up to ~40 miles Petrol engine engages seamlessly once battery depletes or under heavy acceleration Specialist HV diagnostics required for any powertrain replacement ensuring ECU sync Found In: NX450h+ (AZ20, 2021–present) – A25A-FXS PHEV RX450h+ (AL20, 2022–present) – A25A-FXS PHEV (UK launch 2022) Important Notes (Brand‑wide): Hybrid engine replacement is rare but available from specialist suppliers with HV certification Always request a hybrid system health report alongside the engine quote to avoid post‑fitment faults Battery pack health assessment recommended before committing to any hybrid powertrain replacement

Common Lexus Electric Motors: N/A – Pure electric powertrains not yet available in UK Lexus range How Lexus Electric Powertrains Work: N/A – No pure BEV models currently sold in UK Lexus lineup Future models expected to use Toyota e‑TNGA platform with dual‑motor AWD capability Motor failure is extremely rare; battery/module service is the more common specialist requirement Found In: N/A – Pure electric Lexus models not yet available in UK market Important Notes: For hybrid‑electric Lexus models, see the Hybrid section above for replacement guidance Pure electric vehicle enquiries will be referred to Toyota/Lexus authorised HV specialists Labour rates and diagnostic requirements differ significantly from combustion engine work

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Lexus Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Lexus engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Lexus specialists.

entries.title: A25A-FKS 2.5L Petrol Engine
entries.description: This direct-injection four-cylinder powers the NX 250, ES 250, and UX 250 models from 2019 onward. High-mileage UK examples frequently develop intake valve carbon deposits and VVT-i actuator rattle, which degrade performance and increase oil consumption. Our UK-rebuilt units include ultrasonic-cleaned ports, revised cam phaser assemblies, and low-tension piston rings to restore factory efficiency.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A25A-FKS Lexus engine replacement

entries.title: A25A-FXS 2.5L Hybrid Engine
entries.description: Found across the ES 300h, NX 300h, RX 350h, and LS 500h hybrid lineup from 2018 to present. Replacement demand is primarily driven by electric water pump seal degradation and hybrid inverter synchronization faults that trigger limp mode. Quality rebuilt assemblies feature OEM-spec ceramic-seal coolant pumps, recalibrated inverter sync modules, and upgraded thermal management gaskets.
entries.cta: Compare A25A-FXS Lexus hybrid engine prices

entries.title: M15A-FXS 1.5L Hybrid Engine
entries.description: Installed in the UX 250h and newer Yaris Cross hybrid variants from 2020 onwards. While mechanically robust, early UK registrations experienced MG motor resolver sensor drift and hybrid battery cooling fan failures. Specialist rebuilt packages include precision-mapped resolver sensors, reinforced cooling circuit housings, and calibrated ECU mapping for seamless electric-petrol transition.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt M15A-FXS Lexus hybrid engines

entries.title: 2GR-FKS 3.5L V6 Petrol Engine
entries.description: This naturally aspirated V6 powered the RX 350, IS 350, GS 350, and ES 350 from 2012 to 2020. UK owners commonly seek replacements due to plastic water pump impeller cracking and timing chain guide wear past 90,000 miles. Our network supplies units with aluminium water pump housings, reinforced composite chain guides, and pre-installed updated tensioner assemblies.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2GR-FKS Lexus V6 engine replacement

entries.title: 2GR-FXE 3.5L V6 Hybrid Engine
entries.description: Used in the RX 450h, LC 500h, and ES 300h (V6 hybrid) from 2009 to 2022. The primary failure trigger is transaxle bearing wear and hybrid coolant line internal corrosion leading to inverter overheating. Rebuilt units arrive with upgraded sealed bearings, stainless-steel coolant transfer lines, and full MG motor calibration certificates.

entries.title: 3GR-FSE 3.0L V6 Petrol Engine
entries.description: Fitted to the IS 300 and GS 300 (XE20/S190 chassis) between 2005 and 2013. Cold-start timing rattle and VVT-i gear failure from delayed oil pressure buildup are the most frequent replacement drivers for UK owners. Specialist rebuilds incorporate dual-mass VVT-i pulleys, high-flow oil pump rotors, and revised timing chain kits.

entries.title: 2UR-GSE 5.0L V8 Petrol Engine
entries.description: The high-revving powerplant for the LC 500, RC F, and IS F saloon from 2007 to present. Extreme performance driving can cause piston skirt scoring and valvetrain lash adjuster wear, requiring full machining. Premium rebuilt units feature low-friction piston coatings, precision-ground shim buckets, and balanced crankshaft journals for track-ready reliability.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2UR-GSE Lexus V8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3UR-FE 5.7L V8 Petrol Engine
entries.description: Reserved exclusively for the LX 570 flagship SUV from 2007 to 2021. Heavy towing and off-road thermal stress often lead to valve cover gasket seepage and secondary air injection pump bearing failure. Our replacement inventory includes silicone-reinforced gasket sets, upgraded air pump bearing assemblies, and reinforced oil pan baffles.

entries.title: 2AR-FSE 2.5L Petrol Engine
entries.description: Widely used in the IS 250, GS 250, and ES 250 across 2006 to 2016 model years. The most common catastrophic failure is hydraulic timing chain tensioner starvation, which causes chain stretch and potential valve timing drift. Rebuilt engines feature uprated tensioner pistons, revised chain rails, and high-viscosity oil circuit modifications for cold-start protection.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2AR-FSE Lexus 2.5 engine replacement

entries.title: 8AR-FTS 2.0L Turbo Petrol Engine
entries.description: Powers the NX 200t, RC 200t, and IS 300 from 2014 to 2019. Turbocharger oil feed line carbonisation and high-pressure fuel injector tip coking are the leading causes of power loss and replacement requests. Our UK-sourced rebuilds include walnut-blasted intake ports, revised turbo feed filters, and ultrasonic-cleaned direct injection nozzles.

entries.title: 8NR-FTS 1.2L Turbo Petrol Engine
entries.description: Found in the UX 200 and earlier C-HR crossover models from 2018 to 2020. Piston ring carbonisation leading to excessive oil burn and intercooler condensate ingestion are well-documented UK replacement triggers. Specialist units arrive with low-tension oil control rings, upgraded PCV separator valves, and reinforced intercooler drain channels.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt 8NR-FTS Lexus small turbo engines

entries.title: 1AR-FE 2.7L Petrol Engine
entries.description: Fitted to the RX 270, NX 200, and early ES 270 from 2009 to 2015. Water pump bearing seizure and thermostat housing warping frequently cause sudden overheating and subsequent head gasket compromise. Quality rebuilt blocks feature ceramic-seal water pump bearings, reinforced aluminium thermostat housings, and multi-layer steel head gaskets.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1AR-FE Lexus 2.7 engine replacement

entries.title: 1AD-FTV 2.0L Diesel Engine
entries.description: Installed in the CT 200d, IS 200d, and NX 200d from 2008 to 2017. Internal EGR cooler cracking combined with urban DPF clogging accelerates thermal stress and head gasket failure. Our diesel rebuilds include revised metallurgy EGR coolers, updated DPF pressure mapping software, and reinforced cylinder head dowels.

entries.title: 2AD-FHV 2.2L D-CAT Diesel Engine
entries.description: Powers the IS 220d, RX 220d, and NX 220d between 2005 and 2015. The most severe failure mode is turbocharger oil seal blowout under sustained boost, often compounded by injector sleeve leakage. Replaced units arrive with balanced hybrid turbo assemblies, copper-sealed injector sleeves, and upgraded oil return line filters.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2AD-FHV Lexus 2.2D engine replacement

entries.title: 1NZ-FXE 1.5L Hybrid Engine
entries.description: The original Atkinson-cycle hybrid block for the CT 200h and Auris Hybrid from 2011 to 2019. Hybrid inverter capacitor fatigue and MG1 motor bearing wear are the primary reasons owners request full powertrain swaps. Specialist rebuilds feature upgraded DC-link capacitors, sealed MG motor bearings, and factory-calibrated transaxle synchronisers.
entries.cta: Compare 1NZ-FXE Lexus CT hybrid engine prices

entries.title: 2ZR-FXE 1.8L Hybrid Engine
entries.description: Used in the CT 200h, ES 300h, and NX 300h across 2010 to 2020 model years. Electric coolant pump degradation and hybrid battery contactor pitting cause repeated limp-mode warnings and eventual system shutdown. Our rebuilt packages include high-flow electric pumps, gold-plated battery contactors, and complete thermal management calibration. Closing line (Block A): Can’t find your engine code listed above? engineYears.h2: Lexus Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers Lexus's complete UK engine timeline from 1998 to the present across every major model line - from the heritage 1UZ-FE V8 era and the pioneering hybrid powertrain firsts, through the 2GR-FE V6 family, the 2UR-GSE performance V8, and the current Multi-Stage Hybrid and PHEV systems - so owners can identify exactly what was fitted to their vehicle by year and compare rebuilt Lexus engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: 1UZ-FE V8 Dominance & RX 300 UK Arrival 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: Lexus's UK range was built almost entirely around the 1UZ-FE all-aluminium naturally aspirated V8 - one of the most celebrated engines of the 1990s - alongside the 1MZ-FE V6 that underpinned the groundbreaking RX 300 crossover. Key Changes 1UZ-FE 4.0 V8 continues as the definitive Lexus flagship engine across LS 400, GS 400, SC 400 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction RX 300 arrives in UK (1998–1999) with 1MZ-FE 3.0 V6 - first luxury crossover SUV in UK market IS 200 launched (1999) with 1G-FE 2.0 inline-six and 2JZ-GE 3.0 inline-six in IS 300 variant GS 300 continues with 2JZ-GE 3.0 inline-six - naturally aspirated, DOHC, VVT-i Euro 2/3 compliance in force across the range Main Engines 1UZ-FE - 4.0 naturally aspirated V8 (LS 400, GS 400, SC 400) 1MZ-FE - 3.0 V6 DOHC VVT-i (RX 300, ES 300) 2JZ-GE - 3.0 inline-six naturally aspirated (GS 300, IS 300) 1G-FE - 2.0 inline-six DOHC (IS 200) Popular Models Lexus LS 400 Lexus GS 400 / GS 300 Lexus RX 300 Lexus IS 200 / IS 300 Lexus SC 400 Known For 1UZ-FE is widely celebrated as one of the most reliable and long-lived V8 engines ever produced - documented examples with over one million miles on the original block exist; valve cover gasket seepage from 150,000 miles is the only commonly cited maintenance item 1MZ-FE V6 is durable and refined but pre-2000 units on the RX 300 developed an oil sludge issue caused by poor PCV system ventilation - Toyota/Lexus acknowledged the defect and revised the PCV design; flush and synthetic oil switch resolves the issue on affected cars 2JZ-GE inline-six in GS 300 and IS 300 is considered a heritage-grade engine - torquey, refined, and robust; timing belt service at correct intervals is the critical maintenance requirement as failure is catastrophic IS 200 1G-FE inline-six is smooth and characterful but generates very modest power - replacement demand is minimal as the engines are extremely long-lived [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 1UZ-FE, 1MZ-FE, 2JZ-GE, 1G-FE, 3UZ-FE (LS 430 from 2000)
years.cta: Find rebuilt 1998–2002 Lexus 1UZ-FE V8 and 1MZ-FE V6 engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: LS 430 Peak, GS/IS Refresh & RX 330 Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The third-generation LS 430 and second-generation GS and RX solidified Lexus's UK luxury credentials with the 3UZ-FE V8 and updated 3MZ-FE V6, as the brand prepared to launch its landmark hybrid technology. Key Changes 3UZ-FE 4.3 V8 replaces 1UZ-FE across LS 430 and GS 430 (introduced 2000, fully dominant by 2003) RX 330 launched (2003) with enlarged 3MZ-FE 3.3 V6 replacing 1MZ-FE 3.0 - 230 hp GX 470 (2003) introduced with 2UZ-FE 4.7 V8 - body-on-frame SUV for UK market IS 300 continues with 2JZ-GE; GS 300 updated with 3GR-FSE for next generation Preparation for RX 400h hybrid announced - Lexus first hybrid SUV Main Engines 3UZ-FE - 4.3 V8 naturally aspirated (LS 430, GS 430, SC 430) 3MZ-FE - 3.3 V6 (RX 330, ES 330) 2UZ-FE - 4.7 V8 (GX 470, LX 470) 2JZ-GE - 3.0 inline-six (IS 300 final, GS 300) 1G-FE - 2.0 inline-six (IS 200 continued) Popular Models Lexus LS 430 Lexus RX 330 Lexus GS 430 / GS 300 Lexus SC 430 Lexus GX 470 Known For 3UZ-FE V8 in LS 430 is a precision-engineered masterpiece of naturally aspirated refinement - consistently cited by UK Lexus specialists as one of the most dependable large-displacement V8s in any luxury car; timing belt service is the key maintenance requirement SC 430 3UZ-FE powered grand tourer commands a dedicated UK following - engine longevity near-unlimited with correct servicing but valve cover gaskets and coolant hoses are routine maintenance items from 100,000 miles RX 330 3MZ-FE broadly reliable but pre-2004 VVT-i actuator wear is a documented concern - rattling on cold start is the most common UK specialist diagnosis GX 470 2UZ-FE V8 extremely robust - used extensively in commercial and off-road applications globally; replacement demand very low compared to IS and GS models [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 3UZ-FE, 3MZ-FE, 2UZ-FE, 2JZ-GE, 1G-FE, 1MZ-FE (RX early UK stock)
years.cta: Compare 2003–2005 Lexus LS 430, GS, and RX engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: RX 400h Hybrid World First, IS/GS New Generation & 3GR/2GR Introduction 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction 🔴 Technology Introduction
years.description: Short intro: 2005–2006 was the most significant period in Lexus engine history since the brand's founding - the RX 400h introduced the world's first luxury hybrid SUV, the GR V6 family replaced all previous six-cylinder units, and the new IS and GS were the most dynamically complete Lexus models to date. Key Changes RX 400h launched (2005) - world's first luxury hybrid SUV; 3MZ-FE 3.3 V6 + dual electric motors; Lexus Hybrid Drive 🔴 Technology Introduction GR engine family introduced: 3GR-FSE 3.0 V6 (GS 300), 4GR-FSE 2.5 V6 (IS 250) - direct-injection DOHC V6 replaces all previous inline-six and V6 units 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction IS 250 launched (2005) with 4GR-FSE 2.5 - first IS with D-4S dual-injection; IS 350 with 2GR-FSE 3.5 GS 430 continues with 3UZ-FE; GS 460 introduced with 1UR-FSE 4.6 V8 (2007) RX 350 launched (2007) with 2GR-FE 3.5 V6 - replaces 3MZ-FE 3.3 Main Engines 3GR-FSE - 3.0 V6 D-4S direct injection (GS 300) 4GR-FSE - 2.5 V6 D-4S direct injection (IS 250) 2GR-FSE / 2GR-FE - 3.5 V6 (IS 350, RX 350, GS 350) 3MZ-FE - 3.3 V6 hybrid (RX 400h - combustion side) 1UR-FSE - 4.6 V8 direct injection (GS 460, LS 460 from 2007) 3UZ-FE - 4.3 V8 continued (GS 430 final, SC 430) Popular Models Lexus RX 400h Lexus IS 250 / IS 350 Lexus GS 300 / GS 350 Lexus LS 460 Lexus SC 430 Known For RX 400h is a genuine engineering landmark - the world's first production luxury hybrid SUV; combustion engine reliability excellent but nickel-metal hydride hybrid battery degradation after 10–12 years is the primary UK independent specialist concern 4GR-FSE IS 250 direct-injection engine suffers from intake valve carbon build-up - a characteristic of all D-4S early direct-injection units; symptoms include rough cold idle and loss of power from 50,000–80,000 miles; walnut-blasting intake clean-up is the standard UK specialist remedy 2GR-FE/FSE 3.5 V6 family is one of the most respected engines Lexus has produced - extremely long-lived, high torque, smooth; VVT-i actuator wear on cold start is the most commonly cited issue across IS 350, GS 350, and RX 350 from 100,000 miles GS 460 1UR-FSE V8 refined and powerful but relatively rare in UK market - limited specialist availability for replacement [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 3GR-FSE, 4GR-FSE, 2GR-FSE, 2GR-FE, 3MZ-FE, 1UR-FSE, 3UZ-FE, RX 400h Hybrid Drive
years.cta: Source 2005–2007 Lexus IS 250, GS, and RX hybrid engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: IS-F V8 Performance Launch, RX 450h & CT 200h Hybrid Expansion 🔴 Performance Landmark 🔴 Technology Introduction
years.description: Short intro: 2008 brought the IS-F and its 2UR-GSE 5.0 V8 as Lexus's first dedicated performance model - a landmark in the brand's history - while the RX 450h and CT 200h extended hybrid technology across the range to give Lexus a hybrid answer in almost every segment. Key Changes IS-F launched (2008) with 2UR-GSE 5.0 V8 416 hp - Lexus's first F-brand performance model; world's first mass-production V8 sedan-class car with 8-speed torque converter 🔴 Performance Landmark RX 450h (2009) replaces RX 400h - 2GR-FXE 3.5 V6 Atkinson + stronger dual electric motors; EU6-compliant from launch CT 200h (2011, announced 2010) - premium compact hybrid; 2ZR-FXE 1.8 Atkinson cycle engine + NiMH battery Third-generation LS 600h (2007 launch, dominant from 2008) - 2UR-FSE 5.0 V8 + full hybrid; class-leading technology GS 450h continues with 3GR-FSE + rear electric motor hybrid Main Engines 2UR-GSE - 5.0 V8 naturally aspirated 416 hp (IS-F) 2GR-FXE - 3.5 V6 Atkinson cycle (RX 450h combustion side) 2ZR-FXE - 1.8 Atkinson cycle petrol (CT 200h) 2UR-FSE - 5.0 V8 (LS 600h combustion side) 4GR-FSE - 2.5 V6 continued (IS 250) 2GR-FSE - 3.5 V6 continued (IS 350, GS 350, RX 350) Popular Models Lexus IS-F Lexus RX 450h Lexus CT 200h Lexus LS 600h Lexus IS 250 / IS 350 Known For 2UR-GSE V8 in the IS-F is one of Lexus's most celebrated precision-engineered performance engines - Yamaha-designed top end, exceptional high-rpm character; valley plate coolant leak is the one documented failure mode, typically from 80,000–120,000 miles; community consensus is that it is effectively "bulletproof" with correct maintenance CT 200h 2ZR-FXE hybrid is Lexus's best-selling UK model for several years - NiMH hybrid battery degradation after 8–10 years is the primary UK specialist concern, with battery packs typically failing around 100,000–130,000 miles RX 450h 2GR-FXE is refined and impressively economical - hybrid battery conditioning issues in cold UK winters and inverter coolant pump wear from 80,000 miles are the two most reported independent specialist jobs LS 600h remains the most technically complex Lexus ever sold in the UK - the full hybrid V8 system is exceptional but specialist-grade expertise required for independent maintenance [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 2UR-GSE, 2GR-FXE, 2ZR-FXE, 2UR-FSE, 4GR-FSE, 2GR-FSE, 2GR-FE, CT 200h Hybrid Drive
years.cta: Get 2008–2010 Lexus IS-F, RX 450h, and CT 200h engine quotes from UK specialists

years.preview: NX Turbo Preview, GS Fourth Generation & IS Third Generation
years.description: Short intro: The fourth-generation GS and third-generation IS brought the refined 2GR-FSE family to its peak application while setting the stage for the turbocharged four-cylinder that would arrive in the NX, marking Lexus's cautious but significant move away from naturally aspirated engines. Key Changes GS fourth generation (2012) launched with 2GR-FSE 3.5 V6 (GS 350) and 2AR-FSE 2.5 V6 hybrid (GS 300h) IS third generation (2013) launched with 4GR-FSE 2.5 V6 (IS 250), 2GR-FSE 3.5 (IS 350), and 2AR-FSE 2.5 hybrid (IS 300h) LS fourth generation (2013) with 1UR-FSE 4.6 V8 (LS 460) and 2UR-FSE 5.0 hybrid (LS 600h) continues NX 200t (2014 preview/2015 launch) - first Lexus with turbocharged engine, 8AR-FTS 2.0 T-GDi 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction (previewed) CT 200h continues with 2ZR-FXE - highest UK volume Lexus model Main Engines 2GR-FSE - 3.5 V6 D-4S (GS 350, IS 350) 4GR-FSE - 2.5 V6 D-4S (IS 250) 2AR-FSE - 2.5 Atkinson petrol hybrid (GS 300h, IS 300h) 2ZR-FXE - 1.8 Atkinson cycle (CT 200h continued) 1UR-FSE - 4.6 V8 (LS 460) Popular Models Lexus CT 200h Lexus IS 250 / IS 300h / IS 350 Lexus GS 300h / GS 350 Lexus LS 460 Lexus RX 450h Known For IS 300h 2AR-FSE Atkinson hybrid is the dominant UK choice in the IS range by 2013–2015 - efficient and practical; however Atkinson-cycle engines do not fully warm up on short journeys, creating oil dilution concerns in cold UK urban use CT 200h 2ZR-FXE continues to generate the highest volume of UK Lexus replacement enquiries - hybrid battery degradation on 2011–2014 cars is the primary driver; first-generation pack replacement costs range from £1,500–£3,500 at UK independents 2GR-FSE V6 in IS 350 and GS 350 considered best-in-class for naturally aspirated V6 refinement - specialist community considers these among the last great non-turbo performance saloon engines available in this segment 4GR-FSE IS 250 intake carbon build-up remains an ongoing concern for owners who have not addressed the issue with periodic walnut blasting - resale values of affected high-mileage examples are suppressed [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 2GR-FSE, 4GR-FSE, 2AR-FSE, 2AR-FXE, 2ZR-FXE, 1UR-FSE, 2UR-FSE
years.cta: Compare 2011–2013 Lexus IS, GS, and CT 200h engine prices from UK rebuilders

years.preview: NX Turbo, RC-F V8 & Multi-Stage Hybrid Introduction 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction 🔴 Performance Landmark
years.description: Short intro: The NX 200t brought Lexus's first turbocharged engine to market - a significant departure from its naturally aspirated philosophy - while the RC-F launched the 2UR-GSE V8 as a standalone performance coupé and the LC 500 was previewed. Key Changes NX 200t (2015) launched with 8AR-FTS 2.0 turbocharged petrol - Lexus's first turbocharged engine in volume production 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction NX 300h launched simultaneously with 2AR-FXE 2.5 Atkinson hybrid RC-F launched (2014) with 2UR-GSE 5.0 V8 477 hp - second F-brand performance model; items.question: How much does a Lexus engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Reconditioned Lexus engines typically range from £1,800–£4,500 depending on displacement and complexity, with rebuilt units featuring upgraded components (e.g., revised piston rings for 2AR-FSE) costing £2,200–£6,000+. Fitting adds £600–£1,500 depending on model; hybrid variants like the A25A-FXS require specialist calibration. We always recommend comparing three quotes, as prices can vary significantly between suppliers for the same engine code. Compare Lexus engine replacement prices now.

items.question: What affects the cost of a Lexus engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors drive pricing: engine size (1.8L hybrid vs 5.0L V8), condition (used vs reconditioned), vehicle complexity (hybrid inverter sync calibration adds labour), and supply-only vs supply-and-fit. Location matters too — London fitting costs run 20–30% higher than regional specialists. Our comparison tool provides transparent, like-for-like quotes so you see exactly what's included. Get instant quotes from UK Lexus engine specialists.
items.cta: Compare Lexus engine prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Lexus?
items.answer: In most cases, yes — especially if the chassis, gearbox and interior remain in good condition. Rebuilt Lexus engines with upgraded internals (e.g., reinforced timing tensioners on 2GR-FKS V6s) often outlast the original specification. The alternative — purchasing a replacement vehicle — carries depreciation risk and unknown history. If your Lexus holds strong residual value (RX, NX, IS), engine replacement is almost always the smarter financial choice. Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Lexus engine makes financial sense.

items.question: What's included in a Lexus engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply-and-fit service covers sourcing the correct engine code, removing the failed unit, installing the replacement with new fluids and filters, performing hybrid system calibration where required, road testing, and responsible disposal of the old engine. Labour typically adds £600–£1,500 depending on model complexity; hybrid variants like the RX450h require additional inverter sync time. Always confirm whether VAT, delivery and post-fit diagnostics are included in your quote. Compare Lexus engine supply and fit deals.
items.cta: Get a Lexus engine quote now

items.question: How long does a Lexus engine replacement take?
items.answer: Common models like the IS250 or NX200t typically take 3–5 working days from engine arrival to handover. More complex hybrids (RX450h, LS600h) or performance V8s (LC500) may require 5–7 days due to inverter calibration and extended testing protocols. The biggest variable is engine availability — our network prioritises commonly replaced codes like A25A-FXS and 2GR-FKS. Ask suppliers about current lead times when requesting your quote. items.question: Do reconditioned Lexus engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes — every reconditioned Lexus engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Extended 24‑month coverage is often available on premium rebuilt or remanufactured units. Warranty typically covers major internals: crankshaft, pistons, timing chain, oil pump and cylinder head; hybrid inverter coverage varies by supplier. Always confirm exact terms directly with your chosen specialist before committing. Find reconditioned Lexus engines with comprehensive warranty cover.

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Lexus engine?
items.answer: Used Lexus engines (£800–£2,500) offer lower upfront cost and suit temporary fixes or imminent resale. Reconditioned units (£1,800–£4,500) include new seals, bearings and upgraded components addressing known failure points — like revised piston rings for 2AR-FSE oil consumption — plus warranty protection and 100k+ mile expected lifespan. If your Lexus carries a failure‑prone engine family, reconditioned with upgrades is strongly recommended for long‑term ownership. Compare used vs reconditioned Lexus engine options now.

items.question: What are the most common Lexus engine failures?
items.answer: The most frequent catastrophic failures driving replacement include: timing chain tensioner wear on 2GR-FSE/4GR-FSE V6s (70–110k miles); water pump housing cracks on 8AR-FTS turbo petrols; excessive oil consumption from early-spec piston rings on 2AR-FSE units; EGR cooler internal leaks on 1AD-FTV/2AD-FHV diesels; and hybrid inverter coolant pump failure on A25A-FXS powertrains. Early detection rarely prevents escalation — replacement is typically the most reliable solution. Get quotes for rebuilt Lexus engines addressing these known failure points.
